identity.canonical_title: Relation: R_universe / N, Where R_universe Is the Hubble Radius. for N of Order Unity

theory.canonical_statement: ar momentum; universal gyromagnetic ratio; derivation of Newton’s law from toroidal dynamics; predictions for gyromagnetic resonances. Falsifiability / Test Handle: Measure gyromagnetic ratios of astrophysical bodies and test for universality; derive gravitational force from toroidal averages and compare with Newtonian values. 1.0.38 ID 137: Megastructure Formation Theorem Type: Theorem Formal Statement: Standing wave interference across the universe produces filaments with wavelength $λ$ = $π$ R_universe / n, where R_universe is the Hubble radius. For n of order unity, $λ ≈$ 150 Mpc. These filaments are flux tubes with Tš topology; the predicted cosmic web spacing matches observations. Canonical Registry Vol. 01 46 Trawin Zero Point Contextus Canonicus 1 TZP CANONICAL REGISTRY – ENTRIES 100–149 Interpretive Meaning: Largescale structures in the universe—filaments and voids—arise from interference patterns of standing waves; the resulting spacing corresponds close

For n of order unity. It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from R_u, n, v, r, s, w.
